merger_name: Freudenberg Home and Cleaning Solutions – Nilfisk

acquirers.name: Freudenberg Home and Cleaning Solutions GmbH

other_parties.name: FREUDENBERG HOME AND CLEANING SOLUTIONS PTY LTD

anzsic_codes.name: Other Specialised Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing

merger_description:
Freudenberg Home and Cleaning Solutions GmbH (Freudenberg) proposes to acquire 100% of share capital in Nilfisk Holding A/S (Nilfisk).

Freudenberg supplies manual cleaning tools and other cleaning products in Australia. Freudenberg’s brands include Vileda and Oates and Ansell (which is used under licence). Its products include mops and floorcare, brooms, brushware, cloths and wipes, dusters, cleaning carts and trolleys, waste management, window cleaning equipment, spray bottles, durable gloves, and various cleaning chemicals.

Freudenberg is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Freudenberg & Co. KG, which is headquartered in Germany.

Nilfisk supplies professional cleaning machines in Australia including floorcare machines, vacuum cleaners, pressure washers, related accessories, consumables, machine repair and maintenance services. Nilfisk also has businesses in IVS (industrial vacuum systems) and the food industry (cleaning equipment for the food industry).

Nilfisk is the holding company of the Nilfisk group of companies (the Nilfisk Group). The Nilfisk Group is a global original equipment manufacturer for these cleaning machines.

events.title: Freudenberg - Nilfisk - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Freudenberg Home and Cleaning Solutions GmbH’s (Freudenberg)
proposes to acquire 100% of share capital in Nilfisk Holding A/S
(Nilfisk) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Freudenberg, supplies manual cleaning tools and other
cleaning products in Australia. Freudenberg’s brands include Vileda
and Oates. Its products include mops and floorcare, brooms,
brushware, cloths and wipes, dusters, cleaning carts and trolleys,
waste management, window cleaning equipment, spray bottles,
durable gloves, and various cleaning chemicals. It supplies these
products primarily for retailer customer use.
Freudenberg is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Freudenberg & Co. KG,
which is headquartered in Germany. Freudenberg manufactures its
manual cleaning products overseas and imports them into Australia
through third party distributors and large retailers which on-sell to
end customers.
The target, Nilfisk, supplies professional cleaning machines for
commercial use in Australia including floorcare machines, vacuum
cleaners, pressure washers and related accessories and
consumables. It also provides machine repair and maintenance
services.
Nilfisk is the holding company of the Nilfisk group of companies (the
Nilfisk Group). The Nilfisk Group is a global original equipment
manufacturer for these cleaning machines. Nilfisk manufactures its
professional cleaning machines overseas and imports them into
Australia through third party distributors which then on-sell to end
customers.

While the Parties both supply cleaning products, there is minimal
overlap between the Parties. Freudenberg supplies manual cleaning
products, primarily for retailer customer use, and some professional
use. Nilfisk supplies cleaning machines, primarily for professional
use.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information from third partes, and publicly
available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The Parties are not close competitors, and its products are not
readily substitutable for each other. Freudenberg supplies
manual cleaning products (such as mops, brooms, cloths and
wipes) for both consumer and professional use, whilst Nilfisk
supplies professional cleaning machines (such as pressure
washers, and commercial and industrial vacuum cleaners, for
professional cleaning customers) largely for commercial use.
• Post acquisition, the Parties would continue to face competition
from alternative suppliers of manual cleaning products and
professional cleaning machines in Australia, including several
competitors which are of a similar size, or larger than the
Parties.
• While some market participants suggested that the acquisition
could enable the Parties to tie or bundle manual cleaning
products and professional cleaning machines, or foreclose
access to inputs used in the manufacture of cleaning machines,
the ACCC considers that the merged entity would be unlikely to
have the ability and incentive to do so due to the presence of
competitors in both the manual cleaning product and
professional cleaning machine markets in Australia.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.
